Patch Set 1:

The patch is ready, but since it depends on LU-3319 server side patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/7933/ ,  I didn't submit it.

Patch Set 1:

If patch is based on LU-3319 patch, then it could be submitted by checking out the other patch and then rebasing this one on top of it. 

That would at least allow the updated patch to start testing and inspection, and land once the base patch is also landed (which is getting close).

Patch Set 7:

Okay I see what the problem is. For import entry it will only have data only for one item but this is not the case for export. In the case of multiple devices on one node then the export proc file should have multiple entries.

(6 comments)

It would be good to revive this for the 2.10 release so that we can finally get it landed.  It is possible to have multiple data sets included in a single YAML file as long as each section has a unique identifier (i.e. the UUID):

3e689c09-c9f3-e74e-5d74-786590c1dd60:
    name: myth-OST0000
    client: 192.168.10.159@tcp
    connect_flags: [ write_grant, server_lock, version, request_portal, truncate_lock, max_byte_per_rpc, early_lock_cancel, adaptive_timeouts, lru_resize, alt_checksum_algorithm, fid_is_enabled, version_recovery, full20, layout_lock, 64bithash, object_max_bytes, jobstats, einprogress, lvb_type ]
    connect_data:
       flags: 0x4af0e3440478
       instance: 466
       target_version: 2.5.41.1
       initial_grant: 2097152
       max_brw_size: 4194304
       cksum_types: 0x2
       max_object_bytes: 17592186040320
    export_flags: [ replayable, pingable, connect_tried ]
182bfad9-58d7-6573-0c5f-4d0dce4166b8:
    name: myth-OST0000
    client: 192.168.10.159@tcp
    connect_flags: [ write_grant, server_lock, version, request_portal, truncate_lock, max_byte_per_rpc, early_lock_cancel, adaptive_timeouts, lru_resize, alt_checksum_algorithm, fid_is_enabled, version_recovery, full20, layout_lock, 64bithash, object_max_bytes, jobstats, einprogress, lvb_type ]
    connect_data:
       flags: 0x4af0e3440478
       instance: 466
       target_version: 2.5.41.1
       initial_grant: 2097152
       max_brw_size: 4194304
       grant_block_size: 0
       grant_inode_size: 0
       grant_extent_overhead: 0
       cksum_types: 0x2
       max_easize: 4012
       max_object_bytes: 17592186040320
    export_flags: [ replayable, pingable, connect_tried ]

Patch Set 13:

Just a note. This file will be moved to debugfs as it is. Since this is the case it will by default only accessable by root. Are people okay with that?

Patch Set 13:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 13:

James,
Of course I would prefer to keep it world readable, but I guess debugged doesn't have per-file permissions like proc and sys?

Since this is on the server it is less likely to be needed by regular users, but system monitoring tools might want access, and admins may not want to run all their monitoring daemons as root?  It might be a compromise to mount it with group "admin" with group read rights for the whole debuggs filesystem to allow monitoring scripts limited access?

Patch Set 13:

debugfs does have per-file permissions just as you would expect.

The problem is many distros (including redhat) mount debugfs with 0700 permissions at the mountpoint, so it does not really matter what other permissions are further down the path.

Patch Set 17:

Removing erroneous automated rebase review

parents.subject: LU-10003 lnet: clarify lctl deprecation message

commit.subject: LU-3397 lprocfs: create "export" /proc file on server
commit.message:
LU-3397 lprocfs: create "export" /proc file on server

Similar to the "import" file on the client for each client-to-server
connection, it would be useful to have a file on the server in the
per-nid directory obdfilter/*/exports/$NID/export. This contains
export connection information as in the "import" file, like:
 a793e354-49c0-aa11-8c4f-a4f2b1a1a92b:
     name: MGS
     client: 10.211.55.10@tcp
     connect_flags: [ version, barrier, adaptive_timeouts, ... ]
     connect_data:
        flags: 0x2000011005002020
        instance: 0
        target_version: 2.10.51.0
        export_flags: [ ... ]

Also, sanity.sh test_0d is added to verify this patch.

Signed-off-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
